q: What is the point where two rays meet to form an angle called?
sol: The common initial point of two rays forming an angle is called the vertex.

q: How many endpoints does a line segment have?
sol: A line segment is a fixed portion of a line and has two endpoints.

q: An angle whose measure is exactly 90 degrees is called:
sol: An angle of 90° is defined as a right angle.

q: Which of these has no endpoints and extends infinitely in both directions?
sol: A line has no endpoints and can be extended infinitely in both directions.

q: An obtuse angle is an angle that measures:
sol: Obtuse angles are greater than 90° but less than 180°.

q: Two lines that never intersect, no matter how far they are extended, are called:
sol: Parallel lines maintain a constant distance and never meet.

q: The measure of a straight angle is:
sol: A straight angle forms a straight line and measures 180°.

q: How many rays can be drawn starting from a given point?
sol: From a single point, you can draw infinitely many rays in different directions.

q: An angle measuring 45° is classified as:
sol: Angles less than 90° are acute angles.

q: What is a portion of a line that has one endpoint and extends infinitely in one direction?
sol: A ray has one fixed starting point (endpoint) and goes on forever in the other direction.

q: Two angles whose sum is 90° are called:
sol: Complementary angles add up to 90°.

q: What instrument is used to measure or draw angles?
sol: A protractor is the standard tool for measuring the degree of an angle.

q: If two lines intersect at a right angle, they are said to be:
sol: Lines intersecting at 90° are perpendicular.

q: A reflex angle is an angle which is:
sol: Reflex angles are greater than 180° but less than 360°.

q: How many lines can pass through two distinct points?
sol: Only one unique straight line can pass through two specific points.

q: Angles formed by two intersecting lines that are opposite to each other at the vertex are:
sol: These are vertically opposite angles and are always equal.

q: What is the sum of angles in a linear pair?
sol: A linear pair consists of adjacent angles on a straight line, which sum to 180°.

q: An angle of 360° is called a:
sol: One full rotation is 360°, known as a complete angle.

q: Points that lie on the same straight line are called:
sol: Collinear points are points that can be connected by a single straight line.

q: If an angle is 60°, what is its complement?
sol: Complement = 90° - 60° = 30°.

q: How many endpoints does a ray have?
sol: A ray has exactly one endpoint (its origin).

q: Three or more lines that pass through the same point are called:
sol: Lines meeting at a single common point are concurrent.

q: What is the supplement of an angle of 110°?
sol: Supplement = 180° - 110° = 70°.

q: Which angle is equal to its own supplement?
sol: 90° + 90° = 180°, so 90° is equal to its supplement.

q: An angle that is greater than 0° but less than 90° is:
sol: Acute angles range from 0° to <90°.

q: Two angles are adjacent if they have a common vertex and a common:
sol: Adjacent angles share a vertex and one common arm.

q: When a transversal intersects two parallel lines, the corresponding angles are:
sol: Corresponding angles formed by parallel lines and a transversal are equal.

q: A line that intersects two or more lines at distinct points is called a:
sol: A transversal is a line crossing other lines.

q: Which of these represents a point?
sol: In geometry, a fine dot represents a point which has no size.

q: The space between two rays with a common endpoint is called:
sol: The 'opening' between the rays is the angle.

q: How many lines can pass through a single point?
sol: You can draw infinitely many lines through a single point.

q: If two angles are supplementary and one is 40°, find the other.
An angle of 181° is a:
sol: Since it is more than 180°, it is a reflex angle.

q: What is the smallest angle formed by the hands of a clock at 3:00?
sol: At 3:00, the hands are perpendicular, forming a 90° angle.

q: If two lines are parallel to the same line, they are:
sol: Parallelism is transitive; if a||b and b||c, then a||c.

q: A line segment is denoted by:
sol: A bar over the letters (e.g., AB̄) represents a line segment.

q: Which of the following describes a zero angle?
sol: A zero angle occurs when the two rays overlap perfectly.

q: Alternate interior angles are equal when the lines are:
sol: Alternate interior angles are equal only if the lines cut by the transversal are parallel.

q: The interior of an angle is:
sol: The region enclosed within the arms of the angle is the interior.

q: The word 'Geometry' is derived from 'Geometron'. What does 'metron' mean?
sol: 'Geo' means earth and 'metron' means measurement.

q: How many degrees are in two right angles?
In a triangle, the sum of all interior angles is always:
sol: The angle sum property of a triangle states the sum is 180°.

q: Which angle is larger: 30° or its complement?
sol: Complement is 90-30=60, which is larger.

q: A line segment extended indefinitely in one direction is a:
sol: This is the definition of a ray.

q: What is the angle measure of half a rotation?
sol: Half of 360° is 180°.

q: If two adjacent angles are supplementary, they form a:
sol: By definition, a linear pair is two adjacent angles that add up to 180°.

q: Parallel lines are at a ____ distance from each other at all points.
sol: The perpendicular distance between parallel lines is always the same.

q: Which of these is not a type of angle?
sol: 'Linear' usually refers to a pair or line, not a single angle category like acute or obtuse.

q: An angle of 270° is a reflex angle.
sol: Since 270° is between 180° and 360°, it is a reflex angle.

q: Which instrument is used to check if two lines are perpendicular in a geometry box?
sol: Set-squares are designed with 90° angles for this purpose.

q: Two lines in a plane that are not parallel must:
sol: In a flat plane, if lines aren't parallel, they will eventually cross.

q: Find the angle which is four times its complement.
The vertex of an angle lies ____ the angle.
sol: The vertex is the common endpoint of the arms and is part of the angle itself.

q: What is the angle between the North and East directions?
sol: Cardinal directions are perpendicular to each other.

q: Can two obtuse angles be supplementary?
sol: No, because even the smallest obtuse angles (just over 90°) would sum to more than 180°.

q: A line segment has a ____ length.
sol: A line segment has two fixed endpoints, so its length is measurable.

q: Vertical angles are always ____.
sol: Vertically opposite angles are congruent (equal).

q: What is the angle of one-fourth of a circle?
Points that do not lie on the same line are called:
sol: They are non-collinear.

q: Which angle is formed by the hands of a clock at 6:00?
sol: At 6:00, hands are in a straight line, forming 180°.

q: What type of angle is 89.9°?
sol: It is still less than 90°, so it is acute.

q: Two angles whose sum is 180° are called:
sol: Supplementary angles sum to 180°.

q: If two lines intersect, they have ____ common point(s).
sol: Intersecting straight lines meet at exactly one point.

q: The arms of an angle are:
sol: An angle is formed by two rays sharing a common origin.

q: Which direction is opposite to South-West?
sol: Opposite directions are 180° apart.

q: An angle whose measure is greater than 180° and less than 360° is:
sol: This is the definition of a reflex angle.

q: How many degrees are in a full turn?
sol: A full turn or rotation is 360°.

q: A flat surface that extends indefinitely in all directions is a:
sol: In geometry, a plane is a two-dimensional flat surface.

q: Which of the following is correct for a ray AB?
o: Starts at A and goes towards B
sol: In notation AB→, the first letter is the starting point.

q: Two lines in the same plane that do not meet are ____.
sol: Parallel lines never cross.

q: If two angles of a linear pair are equal, each measure is:
What is the measure of an angle that is 1/6 of a straight angle?
A reflex angle of 250° corresponds to an interior angle of:
Which of these alphabets has parallel lines?
sol: The two vertical bars in 'H' are parallel.

q: Angle of 0° is called a zero angle.
sol: Correct. It means no rotation has occurred.

q: The sum of adjacent angles on a straight line is always 180°.
sol: This is known as the linear pair axiom.

q: The edges of a ruler are examples of:
sol: The opposite long edges of a standard ruler are parallel.

q: If a transversal cuts two parallel lines, then interior angles on the same side are:
sol: Consecutive interior angles sum to 180°.

q: How many degrees does the minute hand of a clock turn in 15 minutes?
sol: 15 minutes is 1/4 of an hour, so 360/4 = 90°.

q: An angle is formed by the ____ of two rays.
sol: The union of two rays with a common endpoint creates an angle.

q: A ray has ____ length.
sol: Since it extends forever in one direction, its length is infinite.

q: What is the complement of a 45° angle?
If angle A is obtuse, its supplement must be:
sol: Since A > 90°, 180 - A must be < 90° (acute).

q: What is the measure of each angle in an equilateral triangle?
sol: All angles are equal: 180 / 3 = 60°.

q: A straight line has ____ thickness.
sol: Lines in geometry are idealized as having no thickness or width.

q: Which of the following capital letters has a right angle?
sol: The letter 'E' contains horizontal and vertical segments that meet at 90°.

q: An angle of 135° is:
sol: It is between 90° and 180°.

q: Supplementary angles can both be acute.
sol: False. Two acute angles sum to less than 180°.

q: The measure of a complete angle is ____ times a right angle.
An angle which is half of its supplement is:
Lines which have no point in common are:
sol: They never cross, so they are parallel.

q: What is the angle between the two hands of a clock at 12:30?
sol: The hour hand is halfway between 12 and 1. (630) - (0.530) = 180 - 15 = 165°.

q: Two lines can intersect at most in ____ point(s).
sol: Two distinct straight lines can cross only once.

q: An angle of 91° is acute.
sol: False, it is greater than 90°, making it obtuse.

q: Which is the common part of two intersecting lines?
sol: The intersection point is common to both lines.

q: A square has how many pairs of parallel sides?
sol: Opposite sides of a square are parallel (2 pairs).

q: If three points are not in a line, they are:
sol: They form a triangle.

q: Angle made by the legs of a standard compass when fully open to a straight line is:
sol: A straight line measure is 180°.

q: Complementary angles can both be obtuse.
sol: Obtuse is already >90°, so their sum would exceed 90°.

q: A ray is a ____ of a line.
sol: A ray is a subset or part of a line starting from one point.
